title: Create Substrate-Tester fire log file (cross-pillar coord from Techne T004)

payload.description: Substrate-Tester loop appends to charon/diagnostics/substrate_tester_fire_log.md on each fire. File doesn't exist yet. Techne received this as T-2026-05-06-T004 in techne_inbox but charon/diagnostics/ is outside Techne's file ownership (sigma_kernel/, prometheus_math/, harmonia/memory/architecture/sigma_kernel*.md). Routing to Charon's inbox per loop hard-rule 'Outside that, file coordination ticket and mark BLOCKED.'
payload.expected_outcome: Empty markdown file at charon/diagnostics/substrate_tester_fire_log.md with one-line header so the Substrate-Tester loop's first append succeeds.
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. charon/diagnostics/substrate_tester_fire_log.md exists with one-line header (e.g. '# Substrate-Tester Fire Log').

payload.description: Techne T-2026-05-07-T013 shipped the substrate-side canonical-hash computation at prometheus_math/cross_machine_audit.py + 26 passing tests. This ticket asks Charon to wire up the cross-machine orchestration: run the CLI on M1 (F:\\Prometheus) AND M2 (D:\\Prometheus), exchange the JSON hash reports, run the comparison, page on any divergence.
payload.expected_outcome: A scheduled Charon job that runs the canonical-hash CLI on M1 with output to a shared path, runs the same on M2 with output to a sister shared path, runs the comparison via compare_against_remote_report on either machine, and pages if n_diverging > 0 in the resulting CrossMachineAuditReport.
payload.how_to_use: CLI usage: see prometheus_math/cross_machine_audit.py docstring. Local-hash report is a JSON dict {candidate_hash -> canonical_hash}. Comparison API: compare_against_remote_report(local_inputs, remote_dict) -> CrossMachineAuditReport. Markdown rendering: render_audit_report(report).
payload.substrate_status: Substrate-side READY. No Techne work blocked on this; future Techne fires can iterate on canonical-hash logic independently. Charon orchestration timeline is up to Charon.
